codeReview.summary: The agent-loop refactor itself appears behavior-preserving, but the feature commit is not acceptable as reviewed because it bundled unrelated version bumps into release metadata files.

issues.description: Commit `b2af1aaf5d8acde300b1b777930048db957615a0` bumps the root package version from `0.4.60` to `0.4.61`, which is outside the scope of this non-release refactor and violates the mission guardrail against bundling version bumps with refactoring work.

The same feature commit also bumps the Chrome extension manifest version from `0.4.60` to `0.4.61`, widening scope beyond the agent-loop refactor and changing release metadata without feature justification.

The Firefox manifest version is bumped in the same refactor commit even though the feature only targets agent-loop module splitting; this release-metadata change should have been excluded or isolated.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `refactor-worker` skill already tells workers to diff-check for accidental version bumps, but this worker still staged with `git add -A` and committed `package.json` plus both manifest files. Scrutiny/orchestrator should continue treating version-file diffs as a hard check for non-release refactors rather than trusting handoff claims alone.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for worker session `3045822d-2d66-4095-bb38-e26b591fed0e` shows `git add -A && git diff --stat HEAD` immediately before commit, and commit `b2af1aaf5d8acde300b1b777930048db957615a0` changes `package.json`, `packages/extension/manifest.json`, and `packages/extension/manifest.firefox.json` from `0.4.60` to `0.4.61`.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, mission guidance, and commit `b2af1aaf5d8acde300b1b777930048db957615a0`. The refactor looks structurally sound, but the review fails because the commit bundled three out-of-scope version bumps into `package.json` and both extension manifests.
